finding: The pane reorder handle is a bare draggable <span> with an aria-label but no role/tabIndex/keyboard handler: aria-label on a generic (role=generic) span is not exposed by screen readers (name-prohibited per accname), and pane reorder has no keyboard-operable alternative anywhere (reorderPanes is reached only from the drop handler), so keyboard/AT users cannot perceive or operate this control (WCAG 2.1.1 / 4.1.2).

The in-pane slide-over uses role="region" with a static aria-label="Conversation side panel"; in a split view where two panes each open their right panel, two identically-named region landmarks coexist, tripping axe's landmark-unique rule (the repo's Layer-A axe gate) and giving AT users no way to distinguish the panels — the label should be pane/conversation-specific.

The actual new production wiring `apply_config_limits(&ChatConfig)` (the ChatConfig→ChatStreamLimits mapping that main.rs/lib.rs call at boot) has zero test coverage — every test exercises `set_limits`/`ChatStreamLimits` directly, so a transposition of the two `usize` fields (per_user ↔ global) would compile, pass all tests, and silently mis-apply the caps (e.g. a global cap of 24 = only 24 connections server-wide).

`ChatConfig` applies its numeric caps verbatim with no lower-bound validation/floor, so an operator setting `global_max_connections: 0` (or `per_user_max_connections: 0`) makes `register`'s `inner.clients.len() >= 0` check reject the very first connection, silently disabling all chat streaming with an opaque 429 and no diagnostic.

goTo() calls Stores.Chat.activateBranch (the focused-pane bridge) instead of the render-pane-bound `chat.activateBranch`, so activating a branch in an unfocused pane — e.g. keyboard-activating the prev/next button in pane B (which does not move pane focus, unlike pointerdown) while pane A is focused on a same-conversation compare-branches split — reloads the FOCUSED pane's message window rather than the pane the navigator belongs to, the exact wrong-pane action-routing corruption the added `chat` binding and its own comment claim to prevent; it is also the only converted message-area component that still routes an action through Stores.Chat instead of `chat`.

closePane trims dividerWidths only from the tail (d.dividerWidths.length = panes.length-1) rather than removing the closed pane's gap index, so closing pane 0 or any non-last pane leaves every remaining pinned width associated with the wrong pane; openPane's mid-insert (line 81) and reorderPanes (lines 145-146) likewise mutate panes without remapping the index-keyed dividerWidths.

The cross-device sync:conversation delete handler calls actions.closePane, which (unlike the UI useClosePane path) never collapses a workspace that drops from 2 panes to 1 back to single-pane nor navigates, leaving a degenerate 1-pane workspace with the URL possibly stranded on the just-deleted conversation.

The per-pane extension runtime is built and wired via side effects (new PaneExtensionRuntime, attachExtensionRuntime, setPaneId) inside useMemo; React StrictMode re-invokes the memo factory in dev (and React may discard/recompute memo values generally), creating two runtimes and orphaning the first, risking duplicate/leaked extension wiring.

The loadConversation().then() callback captures conversationId in a closure; if the prop changes while a load is in flight, the stale callback can call Stores.SplitView.closePane(paneId) based on the previous conversation's 404/403 status even though the pane now legitimately holds the newer conversation.

The reactive bridge resolves its target store via the non-reactive focusedApi() (getState of focusedPaneId), so an out-of-pane Stores.Chat reactive consumer subscribes to the focused pane once and never re-subscribes when focus changes, showing stale focused-pane data in split mode and contradicting the bridge's 'reads resolve to the focused pane' contract.

The filtered memo derives the already-open exclusion set from Stores.SplitView.$.panes but omits it from the dependency array (and reads it non-reactively), so the picker keeps listing a conversation that another pane opened after the memo last computed on conversations/query change.

closePane truncates dividerWidths from the tail (length = panes.length-1) instead of splicing out the closed pane's positional index, so closing any pane before a later pinned pane makes every survivor inherit its left-neighbour's pinned width (e.g. 3 panes dividerWidths=[400,500]; close pane 0 leaves [400] so pane 1 renders at 400 instead of its own 500) — should be d.dividerWidths.splice(idx, 1).

The filtered useMemo builds its already-open exclusion set from the non-reactive snapshot Stores.SplitView.$.panes but omits panes from its deps [conversations, query], so if another pane opens/repoints a conversation while the picker is visible the list stays stale and still offers that conversation, causing setPaneConversation's dup-guard to jump focus to the other pane instead of filling this slot.

The 404/403 no-access auto-close calls the raw store action Stores.SplitView.closePane(paneId), which — unlike useClosePane — does not navigate to the survivor or reset when it collapses a 2-pane split to one pane, so if the inaccessible pane was the focused one the URL stays on the deleted/403 conversation and the collapsed single-pane view reloads it, stranding the user on a 404 error state (the exact thing ITEM-29 claims to avoid).

The sync:conversation delete handler calls the raw actions.closePane(paneId); a cross-device delete of the focused pane's conversation in a 2-pane split collapses to single-pane with no navigate/reset, leaving the URL and the resulting single-pane view stranded on the now-deleted conversation.

closePane trims dividerWidths by truncating from the end (dividerWidths.length = panes.length-1) instead of splicing out the removed pane's index; since dividerWidths[k] pins pane k, closing a non-last pane (e.g. the width-pinned leftmost of a 3-pane split) leaves the pinned width mapped to the wrong neighbour pane, which then renders at the closed pane's width.

reorderPanes reorders panes but never reorders the positionally-indexed dividerWidths, so after a header drag-reorder each pinned pane width applies to whatever pane now occupies that index rather than following the pane it was set for.

The filtered useMemo reads Stores.SplitView.$.panes (a non-reactive snapshot) to build the already-open exclusion set but omits it from the dependency array ([conversations, query] only), so the exclusion set goes stale when another pane opens/closes a conversation while the picker is mounted.

In a split new-chat pane (ConversationPickerPane mode==='new' renders <ChatInput/> with conversation null, so modelKey = newChatModelKey(pane.paneId) = '__new_chat__:<paneId>'), selectedModelId is always undefined because loadProviders only seeds the bare NEW_CHAT_MODEL_KEY (ModelPicker.store.ts:95-96) — the dropdown shows the empty 'Select Model' placeholder instead of the deployment default (unlike single-pane), yet composeRequestFields silently falls back to defaultModelId(), so the pane sends with a model that was never displayed.

The sync:conversation delete prune (also AssistantPicker.store.ts:95) only removes real-conversation-id keys, but the per-pane new-chat keys '__new_chat__:<paneId>' the pickers write are never pruned — closePane (SplitView.store.ts:90) doesn't touch selectedByConversation and no delete event carries a synthetic new-chat key — so each split new-chat pane that touches the picker then closes orphans a stale entry, contradicting the comment's 'doesn't grow unbounded / retain stale keys' claim.

After `chat-split-btn`, pane 1 is opened via `openPane({conversationId:null})` and renders `ConversationPickerPane` in 'pick' mode (a conversation list + 'Start a new chat'), which has NO `ullm-model-select`/`model-selector`/message `textarea`; this spec skips the `pane-start-new-chat` click its sibling specs use, so `pane1.getByTestId('ullm-model-select').click()` (and the later pane1 model/draft assertions) target elements that never render — the per-pane composer-isolation claim is never actually exercised.

pane 1 (opened with conversationId:null) shows the empty-pane picker, not a composer, until 'Start a new chat' is clicked; `inputB = pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` then `expect(inputB).toBeVisible()` will time out because the picker's 'pick' mode has only a `conversation-picker-search` input, so the pane-B input-isolation the test claims to prove is never reached.

Same missing `pane-start-new-chat` step: pane 1 renders the picker (no message textarea), so `inputB = pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` and `expect(inputB).toBeVisible()` fail; additionally the file's premise comment ('tab-strip mode was deferred; both panes render as columns at ALL viewports') is stale — `SplitChatView` ships a `!md` tab-mode branch, so the shipped behavior does not match the comment.

This untouched E2E asserts that returning to conversation A shows no assistant chip (toHaveCount(0)) on the stated premise (line 94) that the store holds "a single active-conversation value, not a per-conversation map" — but the reviewed diff replaces selectedAssistantId + the reset-on-conversation-change subscription with a per-conversation selectedByConversation map that PRESERVES each conversation's selection across SPA navigation, so selectedByConversation[convA] is retained, the chip reappears on return, and this assertion now fails.

ModelPicker.store.setState({ ..., selectedModelId: null }) references a state field the reviewed diff removed (renamed to selectedByConversation); the `as never` cast on the object hides it from tsc, so it silently writes a dead key and the fixture's documented "stale selectedModelId → blank render" intent no-ops (the blank now derives solely from providers:[]).

The per-pane subscription (and onConversationLoad at line 93) mutate the GLOBAL Stores.KnowledgeBaseComposer singleton (never migrated to a per-pane store), so in split mode two panes clobber the shared currentConversationId/selectedKbIds — a background new-chat pane wipes the focused pane's KB selection, and attach/detach in one pane persists to whichever conversation loaded last (wrong-conversation data write), despite the comment claiming per-pane binding.

A split new-chat pane keys the selection by newChatModelKey(pane.paneId) = '__new_chat__:<paneId>', which loadProviders never seeds (it only seeds the bare NEW_CHAT_MODEL_KEY), so the model dropdown renders blank in a split 'Start a new chat' pane while composeRequestFields silently sends with defaultModelId() — a display/behavior mismatch and a regression from single-pane, which shows the seeded default.

The added sync:conversation delete-prune only removes real conversation-id keys from selectedByConversation; per-pane new-chat keys '__new_chat__:<paneId>' (paneId is a fresh crypto.randomUUID per pane) are never pruned when a pane closes, so an explicit model pick in a new-chat split pane leaks a map entry per closed pane (identical unbounded leak in AssistantPicker.store.ts:95).

After splitting, pane1 is the empty null-conversation pane which renders ConversationPickerPane in mode='pick' (no composer) per ConversationPage.tsx:541; this test never clicks `pane-start-new-chat` (as the sibling scroll/streaming/adopt specs do) so `pane1.getByTestId('ullm-model-select')` (and later the pane1 textarea/model-selector) does not exist and the whole per-pane model-isolation test fails to run, providing zero coverage of the behavior it claims to prove.

pane1 is the empty picker pane (mode='pick', no ChatInput), but the test asserts `pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` is visible without ever clicking `pane-start-new-chat`, so the composer never mounts and the core per-pane draft-isolation assertion hard-fails / never exercises the claimed behavior.

The test targets pane1's composer textarea to prove Enter-sends-in-the-focused-pane, but pane1 is the null-conversation picker (mode='pick', no composer) and the spec never clicks `pane-start-new-chat` to reach the new-chat composer, so `expect(inputB).toBeVisible()` fails and the focused-pane keyboard-routing behavior is left untested.

The spec's premise is false — it claims tab-strip mode was deferred and asserts both panes render as columns at 390px, but SplitChatView.tsx renders a PaneTabStrip tab mode (ITEM-30, shipped) and its `if (!md)` breakpoint is inverted (md is true when width<=768, so tabs render on WIDE viewports and columns on narrow), so this test locks in the inverted-breakpoint bug instead of the real mobile UX; it also then hits the same picker/no-textarea failure at pane1 (line 46-48).

TEST-P2 titled 'reuses the same window name (dedup, no duplicate)' does not actually prove dedup: the stubbed `window.open` always pushes and returns a fresh handle, so `calls.length` is 2 for the same id and the assertion only verifies the target name is deterministic per id — the reuse/no-duplicate behavior it claims to prove is delegated to the browser and stubbed away.

The 'find opens in pane A only' isolation is asserted against pane1, but pane1 is the empty picker pane which structurally can never render a `conversation-find-bar`, so `toHaveCount(0)` is trivially true regardless of pane-scoping — the negative half of the test proves nothing; a real per-pane-find test needs two conversation panes and a find toggled in one.

After clicking chat-split-btn the second pane is opened with conversationId:null (onSplit in ConversationPage.tsx:143), which renders the ConversationPickerPane in 'pick' mode — it has NO ullm-model-select and NO 'Type your message' textarea until pane-start-new-chat is clicked; this test never clicks it (unlike the sibling specs), so pane1.getByTestId('ullm-model-select').click() (line 54) and the pane1 textarea (line 67) target elements that don't exist, making the per-pane model/draft isolation test deterministically fail rather than prove isolation.

The freshly-split pane 1 is the empty-pane ConversationPickerPane (conversationId:null → pick mode, no composer), but the test never clicks pane-start-new-chat, so `const inputB = pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` matches nothing and `await expect(inputB).toBeVisible()` (line 66) times out — the input-isolation test cannot pass against the shipped picker UI.

pane 1 after split is the ConversationPickerPane (pick mode, no composer) and the test never clicks pane-start-new-chat, so `pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` (line 47) plus the following toBeVisible/fill/press('Enter') target a non-existent composer; the focused-pane keyboard-send test fails before it can verify focus routing.

Same empty-pane-picker mismatch: pane 1 renders ConversationPickerPane (no 'Type your message' textarea — only a 'Search conversations...' input) because pane-start-new-chat is never clicked, so `pane1.locator('textarea[placeholder*="Type your message"]')` at line 46 (and the fill/toHaveValue on it) never resolves.

The test's stated premise — 'the dedicated tab-strip mode was deferred (DRIFT-1.11); SplitChatView renders both panes as columns at ALL viewports' — is stale/false: DEC-49/ITEM-30 shipped a real mobile tab strip (PaneTabStrip, rendered by SplitChatView's `!md` branch), and TESTS.md re-scoped TEST-23 to mobile-tabs.spec.ts asserting a tab strip; this spec asserts columns at 390px and only 'passes' because SplitChatView's `!md` gate (md = width≤768) is inverted (tab mode renders above md, columns at/below md), so the test locks in inverted behavior and never covers the shipped mobile tab strip.

TEST-P2 claims to prove per-conversation window dedup ('the browser focuses/reuses the existing window instead of duplicating'), but the stubbed window.open pushes a fresh handle on every call and never models name-based reuse, so the assertion `calls[0].name === calls[1].name` is tautological (both are derived as `chat-<id>` from the same id) and verifies nothing about the actual dedup behavior it documents.

pane 1 is the empty-pane picker (conversationId:null) whose header has no find toggle and which never mounts a ConversationFindBar, so `expect(pane1.getByTestId('conversation-find-bar')).toHaveCount(0)` is trivially true regardless of whether find state is pane-scoped or global — the find-isolation claim is not actually exercised (it would need a real second conversation in pane 1).

The `if (get().chatStreamClient) return` idempotency guard keys off `chatStreamClient`, but `__destroy__` (store-kit makeBuilder) only runs cleanups + removeGroupListeners and never resets zustand state (grep confirms chatStreamClient is only ever set at :2120 and nulled nowhere); so after the Stores proxy's 5s-grace `executeDestroy` fires (user navigates away from chat >5s, which stops the client via the onCleanup at :2231 and sets storeInitialized=false) and the store re-inits on return, init bails at this line — leaving a permanently STOPPED stream client with no re-registered auth/sync:conversation/sync:reconnect/reconnect listeners, so live assistant tokens and cross-device sync silently die until a full page reload (the old module-singleton design explicitly survived this destroy→re-init, per its own comments).

For a split pane (`ChatPaneStore` = defineLocalStore) under React StrictMode's mount→unmount→mount, the first init registers `onCleanup(() => streamClient.stop())` and the intervening `__destroy__` splices+runs it (stopping the client), but the second init bails at :2097 because `chatStreamClient` is still set in state (never nulled), so the stop-teardown is never re-registered while the pending async IIFE from the first init restarts the same client — on the pane's real unmount `streamClient.stop()` is never called and the SSE fetch/reconnect loop leaks (dev/StrictMode-only, but a genuine per-pane connection leak stemming from the same non-reset-state guard).

initialize()/cleanup() only flip this.initialized AFTER the awaited catalog call, so two concurrent calls on the SAME runtime both pass the idempotency guard and both run initializeExtensions/cleanupExtensions; since the store destroy path invokes cleanup() un-awaited (Chat.store.ts:2258) it can race a loadConversation-switch cleanup (Chat.store.ts:764) on one pane runtime and double-decrement the shared keyboardInitCount (keyboard/extension.tsx:162), and on a workspace hydrated directly into split view (the primary ConversationPane never mounts, so its permanent +1 refcount floor is absent) that can remove the global keydown listener while other panes still need it, killing Ctrl+Enter/Ctrl+K/Esc.

The ChatHookCtx doc comment states it is "passed to the multi-extension send-path hooks (composeRequestFields / beforeSendMessage)", but beforeSendMessage's signature (types.ts:669) takes no args and the registry calls it with none (registry.tsx:734); the pane-scoping ctx added for composeRequestFields is never wired into beforeSendMessage, so beforeSendMessage's per-pane validation silently depends on the focused pane equalling the sending pane rather than the documented explicit ctx.

The pane-bound `chat` handle (line 24) is used for the reactive reads but the branch-activation ACTION still calls `Stores.Chat.activateBranch(conversation.id, branchId)` (the focused-pane bridge) instead of `chat.activateBranch(...)`, so clicking prev/next in a non-focused pane of a same-conversation split activates the branch and reloads messages on the FOCUSED pane — corrupting the other pane's window; this is precisely the bug the component's own comment (lines 21-23) claims to fix, left incompletely applied.

Each per-pane ChatRightPanel instance registers a document-level `keydown` Escape listener when its own in-pane slide-over is open; with two panes' right panels open simultaneously both listeners fire on a single Escape and each calls its own `chat.closeMobileDrawer()`, closing BOTH panes' panels at once — a global-DOM handler that is not actually pane-scoped.

The Escape effect references `chat` in its body but omits it from the dependency array `[inPane, showDrawer]` with no `eslint-disable` (sibling effects added in this same diff carry the disable), so react-hooks/exhaustive-deps will flag it and fail the lint step of the UI gate; runtime-safe only because `chat`'s store ref happens to be stable.

In split view every open pane's in-pane slide-over renders `data-testid="chat-right-panel"`, so with two panels open the DOM contains duplicate `chat-right-panel` testids, breaking any Playwright `getByTestId('chat-right-panel')` selector with a strict-mode multiple-match error.

After `void openConversationWindow(...)` the handler unconditionally calls `closePane(pane.paneId)`, removing the conversation from the workspace even when `window.open` returned null (popup blocked) or the desktop async open rejects — leaving the conversation open in no live surface at all with the failure silently swallowed.

The init guard `if (get().chatStreamClient) return` bails on re-init, but teardown (line 2231) calls `streamClient.stop()` and NEVER nulls `chatStreamClient`, and store-kit's `__destroy__` does not reset state fields — so after any destroy→re-init (the primary singleton when Chat is navigated away >5s then re-accessed, or every split-pane local store under React.StrictMode's setup→cleanup→setup) the re-init returns early leaving a permanently-stopped stream client, and the pane silently receives no further live assistant tokens / cross-device stream frames until a full page reload.

`onCleanup(unsubscribeAuth)` is registered inside the async IIFE after `await import('@/modules/auth/Auth.store')`, so if the store is destroyed before that import resolves (guaranteed for local pane stores under StrictMode, since `__destroy__`'s synchronous `cleanups.splice(0)` runs before the awaited microtask), the later push lands in an already-emptied array and never runs — leaking the auth subscription, which then keeps calling `applyAuth` (start/stop) on a torn-down pane's stream client.

`resetViewState(messageIds)` deletes `collapsed[id]` from the shared global store keyed by message id, but two panes showing the SAME conversation (the compare-two-branches flagship case) share those ids — so when one pane switches/closes and passes its outgoing message ids, it wipes the show-more collapse state the sibling pane is still displaying, defeating the cross-pane isolation the change claims (message ids are unique per conversation, not across panes on one conversation).

beforeSendMessage was NOT threaded with ChatHookCtx (still `() => ...`, and registry.beforeSendMessage() at registry.tsx:723 calls it argument-less) even though the ChatHookCtx doc at types.ts:441 explicitly lists it as a ctx recipient; so every beforeSendMessage impl falls back to the FOCUSED pane (file ext reads Stores.SplitView.$.focusedPaneId, mcp reads bridge Stores.Chat) while the paired composeRequestFields correctly reads the SENDING pane via ctx — in split view a send on a non-focused pane is gated against the wrong pane (pane B blocked by pane A's in-progress upload, or B's queued MCP approvals mis-gated), reintroducing the exact cross-pane coupling ITEM-32's per-pane useSendBlocker fix was meant to remove.

The new keyboardInitCount refcount (the load-bearing GAP-1 fix that keeps the single global keydown listener alive until the LAST pane cleans up) and the PaneExtensionRuntime init/cleanup pairing have no unit test, so any future change that unbalances init vs cleanup (removing the shared listener while panes are still live, or leaking it) would ship silently with no coverage.

onMessageSent clears composerPaneKey(Stores.SplitView.$.focusedPaneId) instead of the sending pane's buffer (get().paneId, which the same send flow threads into composeRequestFields/provideUserContent); because this hook runs AFTER the await ApiClient.Message.send round-trip in sendMessage, the user can click another pane during streaming, so it wipes the newly-focused pane's composer attachments (data loss) and leaves the actual sending pane's files uncleared (re-attached to its next message).

beforeSendMessage gates on isUploading(focusedPaneId) rather than the sending pane, so an Enter-key send from a pane that isn't the focusedPaneId (e.g. keyboard-focused composer) is not blocked while that pane's own upload is in flight — the still-uploading file (absent from selectedFiles/getFileIds) is silently dropped from the message.

onMessageEditRestore restores edit/regenerate attachments into composerPaneKey(focusedPaneId) instead of the pane whose store invoked startRegenerateMessage (get().paneId is available), so if focus differs from the regenerating pane the restored file blocks land in the wrong pane's composer buffer.

focusedPaneRoot() (used by the Ctrl+Enter send, Ctrl+K focus, and Esc clear shortcuts) resolves the target pane from SplitView.focusedPaneId, which is only ever set by pointer-down (SplitChatView onPointerDownCapture / PaneTabStrip onClick) and never by keyboard focus — so a keyboard-only user who Tabs into a non-"focused" pane and types, then presses Ctrl+Enter, sends/clears the OTHER (pointer-focused) pane and loses their typed message; it should resolve from document.activeElement.closest('[data-testid^="chat-pane-"]') instead of focusedPaneId.

The new ChatHookCtx doc comment states the ctx is passed to "composeRequestFields / beforeSendMessage", but beforeSendMessage (types.ts:669) still takes no argument and the registry (registry.tsx:734) calls it with none, so the documented per-pane contract is false — beforeSendMessage cannot scope to the sending pane, forcing the file extension's upload-block check (file/chat-extension/extension.tsx:338) to fall back to focusedPaneId (only coincidentally correct because every send path pointer-focuses the sending pane first).

initialize() and cleanup() flip this.initialized only AFTER their await, so under two concurrent flows on one pane store (e.g. rapid loadConversation A→B switching, or reset() racing a load) an interleaved cleanup() reads initialized===false mid-initialize and no-ops (and vice-versa), leaving the assistant/file per-pane subscribe() listeners double-registered or never torn down and the keyboard keyboardInitCount unbalanced; the flag should be latched before the await or guarded by an in-flight promise.

restoreFromBackup overwrites selectedFiles/uploadingFiles from the backup snapshot but never restores the new fileOwner/uploadOwner maps (and setBackupFiles at line 815 never captures them), so the stream-error recovery path (onStreamError -> restoreFromBackup) re-inserts the sending pane's just-cleared files with no owner entry; composerPaneKey(undefined) resolves them to '__single__', orphaning them from the real pane in split view -> they disappear from FilePreviewList and are dropped by getFileIds(paneKey), i.e. neither shown nor resent (single-pane escapes only because its key equals the default).

onMessageSent clears the buffer with composerPaneKey(Stores.SplitView.$.focusedPaneId) AFTER awaiting ApiClient.Message.send, but the authoritative sending pane is get().paneId (as used by composeRequestFields/provideUserContent); if the user focuses another pane during the in-flight POST, focusedPaneId drifts and clearFiles wipes the wrong pane's unsent attachments while leaving the sending pane's just-sent files in its composer.

beforeSendMessage gates on isUploading(composerPaneKey(Stores.SplitView.$.focusedPaneId)) instead of the sending pane's get().paneId, so it checks upload state of whichever pane is focused rather than the pane actually sending; harmless only while focus is guaranteed to equal the sending pane, which the ctx-threaded composeRequestFields/provideUserContent deliberately do not assume.

The `if (get().chatStreamClient) return` re-init guard bails on the store proxy's routine destroy→re-init cycle (the field is never nulled and teardown already called `streamClient.stop()` at line 2231 + removed the sync listeners), so after the primary Chat store is destroyed 5s after the user leaves chat and then re-accessed, the stream client stays stopped, `applyAuth`/`start()` never re-runs, and `on('sync:conversation')`/`on('sync:reconnect')` are never re-registered — live token streaming and cross-device sync are permanently dead until a full page reload (the old module-scope design existed specifically to survive this re-init).

The new top-of-`applyStreamFrame` guard drops `complete`/`error` frames for a non-open conversation, eliminating the old `clearConversationCache(conversationId)` cleanup for a conversation that completes right as the user switches away (setActiveConversation at line 724 is fire-and-forget `void`, so an in-flight completion frame still lands but is now a no-op); combined with `saveConversationState` caching `isStreaming:true` (line 590) and `loadConversationState` restoring it (line 619), switching back restores a stale streaming snapshot that `reloadOpen`'s `state.isStreaming` guard (line 2137) refuses to reconcile, leaving the conversation stuck showing 'generating' with a partial message and no recovery short of a reload.

onMessageSent clears composerPaneKey(focusedPaneId) instead of the SENDING pane's buffer (get().paneId, which provideUserContent/composeRequestFields correctly use), and it runs AFTER the `await ApiClient.Message.send()` network round-trip — so if focus moves to the other pane during that await (natural in split view: send in pane P, then start working in pane Q), it wipes Q's staged-but-unsent attachments while leaving P's files uncleared (re-attached to P's next message); beforeSendMessage:338 and onMessageEditRestore:307 share the same focused-vs-sending-pane flaw.

restoreFromBackup (and setBackupFiles at 818) snapshot/restore only selectedFiles/uploadingFiles, not the new fileOwner/uploadOwner maps, but clearFiles deletes the sending pane's owner entries during the optimistic send-clear; so a stream-error restore in split mode brings files back into selectedFiles with no owner entry, making composerPaneKey(fileOwner.get(id)) resolve to SINGLE_PANE_KEY and get filtered out of that pane's FilePreviewList/getFileIds — silently dropping the failed message's attachments (single-pane path unaffected).

onMessageSent clears approval decisions keyed by the FOCUSED-pane bridge (Stores.Chat.$.conversation, read at line 1061) instead of the SENDING pane's conversation (composeRequestFields correctly uses ctx.conversationId), and it runs AFTER the awaited network POST (Chat.store.ts:1791→1823), so a pointerdown-focus change into another split pane during the round-trip makes the clear target the wrong conversation — leaving the sent conversation's approval decisions uncleared (re-sent as a duplicate/stale tool approval on its next message) while wrongly deleting the newly-focused pane's queued approval (silently dropping the user's approval), i.e. a wrong-pane approval-routing regression.

The new progress match `!messageId || call.message_id === messageId` silently drops progress for a tool-first assistant turn: mcpToolStart/mcpApprovalRequired stamp `message_id: get().streamingMessage?.id` (extension.tsx:410/517) which is undefined because the handler creates the streaming message AFTER addToolCall, yet the backend progress event carries a real message_id, so `undefined === <uuid>` is false and the live progress bar never attaches (a single-pane regression from the prior server-only match; the fallback only covers 'event has no id', not 'call has no id').

onMessageSent clears approvals under the FOCUSED pane's conversation (`conversation` comes from `Stores.Chat.$` at line 1061, which the chatBridge resolves to the focused pane) rather than the SENDING pane's; because this runs after the `await ApiClient.Message.send()` round-trip, a focus switch to another pane during the in-flight POST leaves the sending pane's queued approval uncleared (re-sent on its next message since composeRequestFields re-reads it) and wrongly drops the newly-focused pane's approvals — the approval is filed/sent under the sending pane (chat.$/ctx.conversationId) but cleared under a different key.

onMessageSent clears approvals for `Stores.Chat.$.conversation` (the FOCUSED pane, read at line 1061), not the SENDING pane — since this hook runs after the `await ApiClient.Message.send()` round-trip and receives no ChatHookCtx, if focus moves to another pane during that await it clears the wrong conversation's decisions and leaves the sending conversation's approval decisions un-cleared, so they are re-sent verbatim on that pane's next message (the send read itself correctly uses ctx.conversationId in composeRequestFields, making this clear inconsistent).

For a tool call that leads an assistant turn with no preceding text content, `get().streamingMessage?.id` is undefined at addToolCall time (the streamingMessage OBJECT is only created later — only streamingMessageId is set on the started frame), so `call.message_id` is undefined; progress events carry the real backend assistant message_id (http.rs forward_progress_notification), so the new `call.message_id === messageId` filter in setToolCallProgress (McpComposer.store.ts:198) never matches and progress updates are silently dropped — a regression that also breaks single-pane (the cited sandbox rootfs-download bar). Should read `get().streamingMessageId`.
fix: Re-found in the round-5 blind audit (MCP #3). `streamingMessageId` is not on the SSE-narrowed `ChatStateForSSE` (only `streamingMessage`), so instead of stamping a real id, setToolCallProgress now treats only a REAL (present, non-`streaming-` placeholder) call id as a usable discriminator and otherwise falls back to server-only matching — so the progress bar never stalls (single-pane fixed) while still scoping per-pane when a real id is present.

finding: beforeSendMessage decides whether to discard the text extension's empty-send cancel using approvals keyed to `Stores.Chat.$.conversation` (the FOCUSED pane), but the approval component sends via the PANE's store; when the approving pane is not focused (e.g. keyboard-activated approve, which fires no onPointerDownCapture to focus the pane) it reads [] approvals, does not discard the empty-text cancel, so chat.sendMessage() throws 'Message send was cancelled' and the optimistic approval reverts — the wrong-pane approval failure the change set out to fix, still reachable for the non-pointer path.

coverageGapsDoc.test.ts (TEST-62) was a self-referential tautology — it asserted the same-commit COVERAGE_GAPS.md contained hardcoded headings/strings the same commit authored, exercising no product code, so it stayed green regardless of split-chat isolation correctness.
fix: Added a doc-vs-repo cross-validation test asserting the artifacts the doc credits (composerOwnership.ts exporting mergeOwnedInto; composer-files-per-pane.spec.ts asserting assistant-status-chip) actually exist on disk with those markers, so the test now fails if the impl/spec is deleted while the doc still points at it.

finding: Module-level recorder singletons (mediaRecorder/mediaStream/chunks/timers/requestGeneration) are shared across per-pane VoiceStore instances; cancelRecording gated only on its own pane's status, so a pane in the ~2.5s post-fail 'error' window (lock already released) that unmounts while ANOTHER pane holds the lock + is recording ran clearTimers/requestGeneration++/stopStream/chunks=[] on the victim pane's live recording and releaseRecordingLock(null) no-oped, stranding the lock forever and permanently disabling every pane's mic.
fix: cancelRecording now short-circuits in the 'error' state to a light cleanup (clear THIS pane's own revert timer + set idle) and never touches the shared recorder that may belong to another pane.

finding: errorRevertTimer was a module-level singleton shared across per-pane VoiceStore instances, so a second pane's fail() cleared the FIRST pane's error->idle auto-revert, stranding it in the red 'error' state.
fix: Moved errorRevertTimer into the per-instance actions closure so each pane owns its own revert timer.

finding: McpMenuItem resolved the conversation from the focused-pane bridge (Stores.Chat.conversation) instead of useChatPaneOrNull() like McpStatusRow, so opening the MCP config modal from a non-focused split pane's + menu pointed the shared modal at the focused conversation (the exact ITEM-47 wrong-pane bug).
fix: McpMenuItem now resolves via useChatPaneOrNull()?.store, mirroring McpStatusRow.

finding: onMessageSent read Stores.Chat.$.conversation (the focused pane) instead of the threaded ownerPaneId, so in split view if focus differed from the sending pane the pending-KB transfer attached to the wrong conversation or silently dropped the new chat's buffered selection.
fix: onMessageSent(ownerPaneId) now resolves the SENDING pane's conversation via paneRegistry.get(ownerPaneId), falling back to the bridge only for single-pane (no paneId).

finding: The pre-existing-approval mount-seed effect read messages but had [] deps, so it ran on bare mount with an empty messages Map and seeded nothing; on a pane remount into split/pop-out with an approval already pending in the process-global toolCalls for this pane's conversation, the scroll effect (which re-runs on messages) could then yank the pane to that pre-existing approval.
fix: The seed now re-runs on [conversation, conversationId, messages] and latches once (didSeedApprovalsRef) the first commit THIS pane's conversation has loaded, marking pre-existing approvals seen before the (later-declared) scroll effect processes them.

finding: For an existing minimized window the override called setFocus() before unminimize(); focusing a minimized window is a no-op on several WMs, so re-triggering pop-out could leave it unraised.
fix: Swapped to unminimize() then setFocus(); the desktop unit test (TEST-75) now asserts the invocation order.

finding: resetPending() clears the single GLOBAL PENDING_KB_KEY slot, and each pane's conversation-change subscription fires it on ANY pane transitioning to a new chat, so two simultaneous NEW-chat panes share/clobber one pending buffer — the new-chat case is not per-pane isolated.
fix: Mirrors the sibling McpComposer's identical single-PENDING_CONVERSATION_KEY model (the pattern ITEM-46 was directed to mirror). Not a regression (pre-ITEM-46 KB had a single flat global selection; ITEM-46 added per-conversation isolation for SAVED conversations). Per-pane isolation of the pre-mint pending buffer would require keying pending by paneId across BOTH KB and MCP for parity — deferred as a cross-cutting follow-up, recorded as FB-11 in HUMAN_FEEDBACK.

finding: loading is a single store-global flag written by loadForConversation for every conversation, so two panes hydrating concurrently race it.
fix: Harmless — no KB component reads `loading` (verified across the diff); recorded for completeness. Would only matter if a per-pane spinner is later wired to it.

finding: ITEM-51 made the MCP pending WRITE per-pane (setCurrentConversation/selectServer via pane-aware resolveConfigKey) but left the primary SEND READ bare-keyed: getSelectedServersConfigFor (called by composeRequestFields with only ctx.conversationId, null pre-mint) read PENDING_CONVERSATION_KEY, so a new-chat split pane's FIRST message either dropped its mcp_config (miss on __pending__ + the empty same-conv fallback) or, when currentConversationId was null, leaked the global selectedServers = whichever pane last opened its modal.
fix: getSelectedServersConfigFor now takes paneId and keys the pending read by pendingConversationKey(paneId); the active-projection fallback is restricted to a REAL conversationId === currentConversationId (never the pending/null case), so a pending pane with no stored config sends [] rather than borrowing another pane's global selection. composeRequestFields passes ctx.paneId (the SENDING pane).

finding: setApprovalMode + toggleAutoApprovedTool created their fallback pending config under the bare PENDING_CONVERSATION_KEY while reading configKey from the now pane-aware resolveConfigKey two lines above — create-key and read-key diverge for a new-chat pane.
fix: Both now create under `configKey` (the pane-aware resolved key).

finding: Follow-on to the HIGH (found while fixing it): applyUserDefaultsToPending + the no-defaults selectServer loop, invoked per-pane by McpInitializer, wrote the new-chat DEFAULT MCP selection to the bare PENDING_CONVERSATION_KEY while a split pane's status row + send path read pendingConversationKey(paneId) — so a new SPLIT pane's default servers would neither show nor send (they landed in the shared key), an ITEM-51-introduced UX regression.
fix: applyUserDefaultsToPending + selectServer accept an optional paneId and write the pane's own pending config (creating it if absent); McpInitializer resolves its pane via useChatPaneOrNull, gates on the PANE's own conversation (not the global currentConversationId pointer), and threads paneId. Null pane → bare key, single-pane byte-identical.
